heading: Federal, State, and local coordination and cooperation
label: 2113 Federal, State, and local coordination and cooperation

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a committee, to be known as the “Forest Resource Coordinating Committee” (in this section referred to as the “Coordinating Committee”), to coordinate nonindustrial private forestry activities within the Department of Agriculture and with the private sector.

children.label: A
children.text: The Chief of the Forest Service.

children.label: B
children.text: The Chief of the Natural Resources Conservation Service.

children.label: C
children.text: The Director of the Farm Service Agency.

children.label: D
children.text: The Director of the National Institute of Food and Agriculture.

children.label: i
children.text: At least three State foresters or equivalent State officials from geographically diverse regions of the United States.

children.label: ii
children.text: A representative of a State fish and wildlife agency.

children.label: iii
children.text: An owner of nonindustrial private forest land.

children.label: iv
children.text: A forest industry representative.

children.label: v
children.text: A conservation organization representative.

children.label: vi
children.text: A land-grant university or college representative.

children.label: vii
children.text: A private forestry consultant.

children.label: viii
children.text: A representative from a State Technical Committee established under .section 3861 of this title

children.label: F
children.text: Such other persons as determined by the Secretary to be appropriate.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 3 Chairperson
children.text: The Chief of the Forest Service shall serve as chairperson of the Coordinating Committee.

children.label: A
children.text: provide direction and coordination of actions within the Department of Agriculture, and coordination with State agencies and the private sector, to effectively address the national priorities specified in , with specific focus owners of nonindustrial private forest land;section 2101(c) of this title1So in original. The word “on” probably should appear after “focus”.1

children.label: B
children.text: clarify individual agency responsibilities of each agency represented on the Coordinating Committee concerning the national priorities specified in , with specific focus on nonindustrial private forest land;section 2101(c) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: provide advice on the allocation of funds, including the competitive funds set-aside by ; andsection 2109a of this title

children.label: D
children.text: assist the Secretary in developing and reviewing the report required by .section 2101(d) of this title

children.heading: Meeting
children.label: 5 Meeting
children.text: The Coordinating Committee shall meet annually to discuss progress in addressing the national priorities specified in and issues regarding nonindustrial private forest land.section 2101(c) of this title

children.heading: Federal members
children.label: A Federal members
children.text: Members of the Coordinating Committee who are full-time officers or employees of the United States shall receive no additional pay, allowances, or benefits by reason of their service on the Coordinating Committee.

children.heading: Non-Federal members
children.label: B Non-Federal members
children.text: Non-federal members of the Coordinating Committee shall serve without pay, but may be reimbursed for reasonable costs incurred while performing their duties on behalf of the Coordinating Committee.2So in original. Probably should be “Non-Federal”.2

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the State forester or equivalent State official of each State, shall establish a State Forest Stewardship Coordinating Committee (hereafter referred to in this section as the “State Coordinating Committee”) for each such State.

children.label: i
children.text: representatives from the Forest Service, Soil Conservation Service, Agricultural Stabilization and Conservation Service, and National Institute of Food and Agriculture;

children.label: I
children.text: local government;

children.label: II
children.text: consulting foresters;

children.label: III
children.text: environmental organizations;

children.label: IV
children.text: forest products industry;

children.label: V
children.text: forest land owners;

children.label: VI
children.text: land-trust organizations, if applicable in the State;

children.label: VII
children.text: conservation organizations;

children.label: VIII
children.text: the State fish and wildlife agency; and

children.label: IX
children.text: the State Technical Committee.

children.label: iii
children.text: any other individuals determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Terms
children.label: C Terms
children.text: The members of the State Coordinating Committee appointed under subparagraph (B)(ii) shall serve 3-year terms, with the initial members serving staggered terms as determined by the State forester or equivalent State official, and may be reappointed for consecutive terms.

children.heading: Existing committees
children.label: D Existing committees
children.text: Existing State forestry committees may be used to complement, formulate, or replace the State Coordinating Committees to avoid duplication of efforts if such existing committees are made up of membership that is similar to that described in subparagraph (B)(ii), and if such existing committees include landowners and the general public in their memberships.

children.label: A
children.text: consult with other Department of Agriculture and State committees that address State and private forestry issues;

children.label: B
children.text: make recommendations to the Secretary concerning the assignment of priorities and the coordination of responsibilities for the implementation of this chapter by the various Federal and State forest management agencies that take into consideration the mandates of each such agency;

children.label: C
children.text: make recommendations to the State forester or equivalent State official concerning the development of the State-wide assessment and strategy regarding forest resource conditions under ; andsection 2101a of this title

children.label: D
children.text: make recommendations to the Secretary concerning those forest lands that should be given priority for inclusion in the Forest Legacy Program established pursuant to .section 2103c of this title

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 3 Termination
children.text: The State Coordinating Committees shall not terminate.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 4 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to compel action by any State official.
